submission_xml.group_contribution: For the entire group, I have contributed to point 2. Use and integration of external API's, part 3. Configuration file and the logical er model. Being in a group of 3 people, we tried to divide the work equally, but it was pretty difficult to do that, especially when not everyone knew how to use sql to create a database, or creating php files. I have more experience using front-end and creating sql's so I took most of the coding & sql parts.

individual_component.type: Document the code and the system
individual_component.description: While creating most of the code, and even going through files which I haven't created, I have mapped out what each sequence does from that part of the code. Although I haven't put explanations everywhere, as in I didn't say for javascript, every php file has deep details for it.
individual_component.problems: I have encountered problems when creating the weather.php file as it was kinda a new thing for me. The problems were relatively normal, as in the API key wasn't connecting, it wasn't gathering the correct information. And finally, it wouldn't find the coordinates
individual_component.solutions: The solution were that instead of trying to read the API Key, instead, I made it only for config.php so that the weather.php file summon it and connect to it. And I also made it so that it gathers the correct information by making it directly connect to places_of_interestID from the database. Finally, to fix the final issue, I made it to gathered the lat and lon specifically from the database.

file.name: weather.php
file.purpose: To fetch and summon all weather criteria.

file.name: index.php
file.purpose: Main page. It's got both of the maps and weather forecast, but it does not have the places of interest. Those are for their own pages

submission_xml.security_discussion:
For our twin cities website, we have found that we do qualify in a couple Parkerian Hexad attributes. One of them is confidentiality. We have decided to not implement a login / logout system. We have decided this for only one truthful reason. We didn't see fit for it. We have implement multiple things that wasn't asked of us, but we did not see any reason to add a login / logout system. This would have made our life harder and there's no point in it. As for our API keys, we decided to make them server-side that they don't become a hazard to us. This way the APIs aren't exposed, and we can use them whenever we press "F5" to run the code. For even further protection, we are 100% sure that all of our database information and even all the work that we've done is saved and completely untouchable on our Github repository. This is being said because we have made our repository private, meaning only the three of us which have created this code can access it, modify it and save it. The contributors are just the three people present in "group L". So not only is our github fully protected, so it's our website when we run it. Being run by localhost it truly means no external access can penetrate or invade our project.
As in for our integrity, all the information displayed on this project is read-only. We do not have any way for user to generate content, or to input it in any way. Not only can the users not generate content, the data shown is also read-only. The data presented on the project cannot be modified or tampered with in any way by any visitors. Even using localhost to publish it, when public, the website cannot be modified in any way. The foreign keys in our database prevent any type of corruption and makes the localhost a completely safe space to work in and use. For our final integrity check, we have made sure that all our PDO statements prevent any type of SQL injection. This was made as the last line of backup to make sure nothing can be edited.
For our authenticity, we have gathered all the information from public sources, such as OpenWeatherAPI, wikipedia, tourism sites, and others. From OpenWeatherAPI, we have gathered all the necessary information for the humidity and weather forecast status. All this information is stored on their part, we get all of this information for simply using their free generated weather_api_key. All the city information, as in coordinates and postcode has been gathered from wikipedia and google search engine. As in for information for the description of each individual place, we have gathered that from the tourism website of each individual city. We also have our limitations. For example : Flickr. Flickr limits us on our sample images. We knew this and yet we still decided to use because it's a great api to use.
As for the final security discussion, we can talk about our availability. Database will always automatically start is the XAMPP and the php files are running. This meaning that the project is limited because of the localhost limitations. But even with this, if our weather_api_key falls offline, we have a fallback data until it starts running again. And even then, we do have our RSS feed as an alternative access method.
submission_xml.individual_comment: My main contributions was in setting up the structure and the weather side of the coding side. For how I found it, I can't really say it was easy. But, I can say that it challenged me and taught me some stuff. I haven't really worked with php in this volume before. So my attitude towards this coding language has changed, for both better and worse. If I were to choose the most difficult part, it would definitely be integrating the javascript with the php code. I have struggled the most at that part of the code and I have been working with javascript for more than 3 years. But even with this experience I have made multiple errors with it, dozen even. Those mistakes made my code crash multiple times and just a few days ago I got it figured out. Honestly, just those 150-200 lines of coding cause me more trouble than the MySQL. Creating the database for the information we require was pretty tough, but at least I had the conceptual and logical component as my go to for inspiration and what we needed. I haven't really gave much stress about learning php. It wasn't hard learning the basics of php, more of a journey I could say. Just 2 tutorials and w3schools was enough to help me get started on this project.
